name: Suvam Kumar Bhola
intro: I am a computer science student passionate about open-source, system design, and building intelligent web applications. Focused on blending minimalist design with cutting-edge engineering to solve real-world problems.

about.shortBio: Computer Science student at GITAM University with a passion for web development and AI.
about.longBio: With a strong foundation in computer science from my diploma and ongoing B.Tech, I have actively engaged in open-source programs like SSOC and GSSOC, and developed real-time web applications. I serve as a Google Student Ambassador, organizing tech workshops and fostering a strong developer community.
about.goals: To innovate at the intersection of web technologies and artificial intelligence, creating impactful products.
about.vision: A digital landscape where every interaction is intelligent, purposeful, and accessible.

responsibilities: Developed AI-based solutions by applying machine learning concepts to real-world datasets and problem statements.
Performed data preprocessing, feature engineering, exploratory data analysis, and model evaluation to improve prediction accuracy.
Trained and validated models in Python using industry-standard AI/ML libraries, applying optimization techniques.
Selected as a contributor for two national-level open-source programs, SSOC and GSSOC, contributing to multiple repositories.
Fixed bugs and implemented new features across open-source projects, improving functionality and code reliability.
Collaborated with maintainers and fellow contributors through GitHub issues and pull requests.
Analyzed a client telemetry dataset using Tableau, identifying patterns in machine performance and operational efficiency.
Processed and visualized 10,000+ data points to generate data-driven insights and trend reports.
Represented Google technologies on campus as a Student Ambassador.
Organized workshops and events on Web Development, AI, and Cloud Technologies.

projects.title: AI-Powered Traffic Detection System
projects.description: A real-time traffic detection web application built in a 4-person team using the MERN stack during a 1-day hackathon. Won 1st Place at the GIET Hackathon.

projects.title: Cosmic Watch
projects.description: NASA NeoWs Asteroid Tracking System integrating real-time near-Earth asteroid data. Includes a risk analysis module and secure JWT authentication.

certifications.title: Microsoft Certified - Web Design and Development
